MaxResults.description: The maximum number of brokers that Amazon MQ can return per page (20 by default). This value must be an integer from 5 to 100.

NextToken.description: The token that specifies the next page of results Amazon MQ should return. To request the first page, leave nextToken empty.

toolSpec.name: update_broker
toolSpec.description: Execute the AWS AmazonMQ `update_broker` operation.

region.description: AWS region on which the broker is in

AuthenticationStrategy.description: Optional. The authentication strategy used to secure the broker. The default is SIMPLE.

AutoMinorVersionUpgrade.description:
Enables automatic upgrades to new patch versions for brokers as new versions are released and supported by Amazon MQ. Automatic upgrades occur during the scheduled maintenance window or after a manual broker reboot.

Must be set to true for ActiveMQ brokers version 5.18 and above and for RabbitMQ brokers version 3.13 and above.

Configuration.description: A list of information about the configuration.

EngineVersion.description:
The broker engine version. For more information, see the ActiveMQ version management and the RabbitMQ version management sections in the Amazon MQ Developer Guide.

When upgrading to ActiveMQ version 5.18 and above or RabbitMQ version 3.13 and above, you must have autoMinorVersionUpgrade set to true for the broker.

HostInstanceType.description: The broker's host instance type to upgrade to. For a list of supported instance types, see Broker instance types.

LdapServerMetadata.description: Optional. The metadata of the LDAP server used to authenticate and authorize connections to the broker. Does not apply to RabbitMQ brokers.

SecurityGroups.description: The list of security groups (1 minimum, 5 maximum) that authorizes connections to brokers.

DataReplicationMode.description: Defines whether this broker is a part of a data replication pair.

toolSpec.description: Execute the AWS AmazonMQ `update_configuration` operation.

region.description: AWS region on which the broker is in

toolSpec.name: rabbimq_broker_initialize_connection
toolSpec.description: Connect to a new RabbitMQ broker which authentication strategy is SIMPLE.

toolSpec.name: rabbimq_broker_initialize_connection_with_oauth
toolSpec.description: Connect to a new RabbitMQ broker using OAuth. It only applies to RabbitMQ broker which authentication strategy is config_managed.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_get_guideline
toolSpec.description: Get the general best practices for deploying RabbitMQ on Amazon MQ.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_list_queues
toolSpec.description: List all the queues in the broker.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_list_exchanges
toolSpec.description: List all the exchanges in the broker.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_list_vhosts
toolSpec.description: List all the virtual hosts (vhosts) in the broker.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_get_queue_info
toolSpec.description: Get detailed information about a specific queue.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_get_exchange_info
toolSpec.description: Get detailed information about a specific exchange.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_list_shovels
toolSpec.description: Get detailed information about shovels in the RabbitMQ broker.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_get_shovel_info
toolSpec.description: Get detailed information about specific shovel by name that is in a selected virtual host (vhost) in the RabbitMQ broker.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_get_cluster_nodes_info
toolSpec.description: Get the list of nodes and their info in the cluster.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_list_connections
toolSpec.description: List all connections on the RabbitMQ broker.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_list_consumers
toolSpec.description: List all consumers on the RabbitMQ broker.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_list_users
toolSpec.description: List all users on the RabbitMQ broker.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_is_in_alarm
toolSpec.description: Check if the RabbitMQ broker is in alarm.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_is_quorum_critical
toolSpec.description: Check if there are quorum queues with minimum online quorum.

toolSpec.name: rabbitmq_broker_get_broker_definition
toolSpec.description: Get the RabbitMQ definitions: exchanges, queues, bindings, users, virtual hosts, permissions, topic permissions, and parameters. Everything apart from messages.
